candidates.name: Kelly Ayotte
candidates.notes: 83rd Governor of New Hampshire (2025-). Roman Catholic. Former U.S. Senator (2011-2017) and state Attorney General. Moderate Republican. As senator had Heritage Action ~60% and NRA A rating. Pro-life but supports exceptions. Voted to repeal 'Don't Ask Don't Tell' and supported some immigration reform. Elected governor defeating Democrat Joyce Craig in 2024.

profile.education: Pennsylvania State University (BA, Political Science); Villanova University School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Attorney; New Hampshire Attorney General (2004-2009); U.S. Senator (2011-2017); became 83rd governor in January 2025.

candidates.name: Jeanne Shaheen
candidates.notes: Senior Senator from New Hampshire (2009-). Protestant. Heritage Action lifetime 4%. NRA F. First woman elected both governor and U.S. senator in American history. Former 78th Governor of NH (1997-2003). Announced she will not seek reelection in 2026. Consistently progressive voting record.

profile.education: Shippensburg University (BA English), University of Mississippi (MST)
profile.background: Former Governor of New Hampshire, first woman elected governor there. Former high school teacher and state senator. In Senate since 2009. Foreign Relations Committee senior member.
profile.office: 506 Hart Senate Office Building

candidates.name: Maggie Hassan
candidates.notes: Junior Senator from New Hampshire (2017-). United Church of Christ / Congregationalist. Heritage Action lifetime ~4%. NRA D rating (2012). Former 81st Governor of NH (2013-2017). Consistently progressive across all categories. Supports abortion access, gun control, LGBTQ+ rights, immigration reform.

profile.education: Brown University (BA English), Northeastern University School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Former Governor of New Hampshire (2013-2017) and New Hampshire State Senator. Education and disability rights attorney. Advocate for children with disabilities.
profile.office: 324 Hart Senate Office Building
